skill.name: evaluation
skill.description: Guidance for building evaluation frameworks, rubrics, test sets, and quality gates for agent systems.
skill.summary: Design practical evaluation systems for AI agents.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported 29 potential issues, but manual review found documentation-only content with no executable shell calls, network requests, cryptographic operations, or prompt injection. The backtick findings are Markdown code fences, the URL is source metadata, and the remaining alerts are terminology false positives.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged backticks as Ruby or shell execution. These lines delimit Python examples in Markdown and do not execute commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are Markdown fence markers around example text. No shell command, Ruby command substitution, or executable wrapper is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Source URL Flagged as Network Behavior
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL is frontmatter source metadata. The skill contains no instruction to fetch, post, download, or send data to that URL.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears only in the metadata block as a repository source. There is no surrounding network call or data transfer instruction.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Evaluation Terminology Flagged as Crypto or Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Static analysis matched ordinary evaluation terms such as system, source, model, tool, and multi-dimensional. No weak cryptographic algorithm, system probing command, or network reconnaissance workflow was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows prose about agent evaluation methods and example test cases. I did not find crypto APIs, hash choices, probing commands, scanners, or host discovery instructions.

content.value_statement: Agent behavior is hard to measure because outputs vary across runs and valid paths. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code design rubrics, test sets, and quality gates for agent systems.
actual_capabilities: Defines multi-dimensional rubrics for agent quality.
Plans stratified test sets across complexity levels.
Compares agent configurations with repeatable metrics.
Combines LLM-as-judge review with human evaluation.
Designs continuous evaluation pipelines and quality gates.
Tracks regressions, token usage, and tool efficiency over time.
limitations: Provides methodology and examples, not a complete evaluator package.
Requires user-supplied test cases, expected outcomes, and scoring weights.
Does not replace expert review for safety-critical agent behavior.
References an internal metrics document that is not included in the scanned file list.

use_cases.title: Create Product Quality Gates
use_cases.description: Define pass and fail criteria before releasing agent changes.

use_cases.title: Compare Prompt Strategies
use_cases.description: Measure how context, prompts, and model choices affect agent outcomes.

use_cases.title: Monitor Production Agents
use_cases.description: Design sampled evaluations that catch quality drops after deployment.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Basic Rubric
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for one agent task.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a simple evaluation rubric for an agent that handles [task]. Include accuracy, completeness, citations, and tool efficiency.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Test Set
prompt_templates.scenario: Build coverage across common and difficult cases.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a test set for [agent workflow]. Include simple, medium, complex, and very complex cases with expected outcomes.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Agent Versions
prompt_templates.scenario: Evaluate prompt, model, or tool configuration changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an evaluation plan to compare [version A] and [version B]. Include metrics, thresholds, sample size, and regression checks.

prompt_templates.title: Build Continuous Evaluation
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced production monitoring and release governance.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a continuous evaluation pipeline for [agent system]. Include sampling, LLM-as-judge review, human review, dashboards, and alert thresholds.

output_examples.input: Design an evaluation rubric for a research agent.
output: Quality dimensions for factual accuracy, completeness, citation accuracy, source quality, and tool efficiency.
Scoring levels for excellent, acceptable, partial, and failed responses.
A weighted pass threshold with guidance for human review.

output_examples.input: Create a test set plan for a support agent.
output: Simple cases for direct policy lookup.
Medium cases that require comparing multiple support articles.
Complex cases with ambiguous requests, missing details, and escalation decisions.

output_examples.input: Plan production monitoring for an agent workflow.
output: A sampling strategy for recent interactions.
Metrics for score trends, tool efficiency, and failure categories.
Alert thresholds for quality drops and repeated failure patterns.

best_practices: Evaluate outcomes instead of requiring one exact execution path.
Use multiple quality dimensions with clear scoring levels.
Combine automated judging with sampled human review.
anti_patterns: Relying on a single overall score without dimension-level detail.
Building test sets only from easy or synthetic examples.
Changing prompts or models without preserving baseline metrics.

faq.question: What kind of agents does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports agents that search, use tools, make decisions, or complete multi-step workflows.

faq.question: Does this skill run evaluations automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides evaluation design guidance and examples, but users must implement the pipeline.

faq.question: Can it help with LLM-as-judge evaluation?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains how to define dimensions, scales, task context, and structured judgments.

faq.question: How should teams choose pass thresholds?
faq.answer: Teams should set thresholds from product risk, baseline scores, and required quality for the workflow.

faq.question: Does it require ground truth answers?
faq.answer: Ground truth helps when available, but the skill also supports rubric-based judgment for open-ended tasks.

faq.question: Is this suitable for production monitoring?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for sampling interactions, tracking trends, and alerting on quality drops.
